rulings.Case Title: DOUGLAS BELL VS. KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JULY 16, 2015 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 1, Defendant AMF Incorporated's motion for summary judgment is continued to July 29, 2015 pursuant to plaintiff's C.C.P. 437c(h) request for a continuance, which is granted. Plaintiff demonstrated that facts essential to justify opposition may exist and explained why those facts cannot now be presented. Supplemental opposition, if any, due by July 22, 2015 at 4:00p.m. Supplemental reply, if any, due by July 27, 2015 at 12:00p.m. If a hearing is requested to contest this continuance, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30p.m. on July 24 stating their intention to contest. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LAWRENCE GRAUBART ET AL VS. AMERICAN CYANAMID COMNPANY AKA WYETH HOLDINGS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JULY 16, 2015 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 2, Defendant Hart Chemicals, Inc. dba Pacific Coast Chemicals Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. As to the issue of sale of talc to plaintiff's employers, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing talc attributable to defendant. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar because it did not demonstrate that plaintiff failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive written discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support such claim. The Court will not consider the written discovery requests submitted with defendant's reply. As to the issue of successor liability, defendant failed to sustain its burden of submitting evidence to negate an essential element of plaintiff's claim. Defendant's evidence does not demonstrate that it is not liable for sales of talc made to plaintiff's employers by a Pacific Coast Chemicals Company entity during the time plaintiff worked in the paint industry. As to the issue of dose and duration of exposure, defendant failed to sustain its burden of submitting evidence to negate an essential element of plaintiff's claim. Defendant's evidence does not demonstrate that any talc to which plaintiff was exposed that is attributable to defendant did not contain asbestos and does not demonstrate that any exposure to such talc did not cause or contribute to the increased risk of plaintiff developing his asbestos-related disease. Plaintiff's objections to the Stull declaration are overruled as to paragraph 9 and sustained as to paragraphs 10, 11 and 12 and as to Exhibits E and F attached to the Stull declaration. Plaintiff's objections to defendant's Exhibit B and paragraph 3 of the Tye declaration are sustained. Plaintiff's objections to the Hayman and Kelman declarations are overruled. Defendant's objections to the telephone directory pages (Exhibit D) are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
